summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacon scripts that exfiltrate host/environment data
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node run.js") that auto-executes on every install. The tarball ships beacon34.js and beacon_linux.js, which together combine child_process, os, http, and https with reads of process.env, process.platform, and os.hostname() — the structural fingerprint of an install-time host-reconnaissance/exfiltration beacon. beacon_linux.js issues outbound http.get and http.request POST traffic; beacon34.js performs multiple GET requests after pulling in child_process and https. Installing this package causes the installer's machine to phone home with system identifiers and environment data, and provides a code-execution surface (child_process) under the installer's privileges before the developer ever invokes the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
